title: TLS Certificate Expiry Checker
description:
Input: lines of `hostname expiry_unix_timestamp`, then `---`, then a reference Unix timestamp.

Find all certificates expiring within 30 days of the reference timestamp. Print `hostname days_remaining` for each, sorted by days_remaining ascending.

Return empty output if none expire within 30 days.

examples.explanation: (1714000000-1713827200)/86400=2 days → flagged. web.example.com=35 days → not flagged.

starter_code.bash:
#!/bin/bash
# Read hostname+expiry lines, then ---, then reference timestamp
# Print 'hostname days' for certs expiring within 30 days, sorted by days asc
ref=""
declare -A certs
reading=true
while IFS= read -r line; do
    if [ "$line" = "---" ]; then reading=false; continue; fi
    if [ "$reading" = true ]; then
        host=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}')
        expiry=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}')
        certs[$host]=$expiry
    else
        ref=$line
    fi
done
# TODO: compute days_remaining for each cert and print those < 30

test_cases.description: One cert expiring soon, one safe
